---
title: "The charge channel, and an honest note on coverage"
subtitle: "T2 · Phase T2.3 · `va-codegen::lower`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: charge channel is real and transient-ready; a dedicated "coverage matrix" was never
built as its own artifact** — the honest state, not glossed over. `docs/token-reference.md` (T1's
own living coverage record) and `docs/roadmap.md`'s dated changelog are where real coverage
tracking actually lives; see "Limitations" for why a separate T2-specific matrix never
materialized.
:::

## Goal

Route `ddt`/`idt` into the charge channel `va-transient`'s companion model needs to integrate a
reactive device in time — and be honest about what "coverage" means for this crate specifically.

## Where it fits

```
 ddt(q)/idt(expr) in the analog block ──► [lower.rs] ──► charge/dcharge stamps ──► va-transient
```

DC analysis (`va-core`) never reads the charge channel at all; it exists purely for T4's
companion-model integration.

## The idea

**`ddt` is recovered symbolically, `idt` is not — architecturally different, for a real reason.**
`ddt(q)`'s value at the current instant depends only on `q`'s *current* value and its Jacobian
(the charge channel's `Q`/`dQ/dV` are exactly `q`/`dq/dx` at this operating point) — `va-transient`
differentiates that in time itself via its own companion model, so `lower.rs` just needs to
recognize the `ddt(...)` shape as a top-level additive term and route its argument to
`sink.charge`/`sink.dcharge` instead of `sink.residual`/`sink.jacobian`.

`idt(expr)`'s value, by contrast, is `expr`'s entire *history* integrated to now — it can't be
recovered from the current unknowns alone. Each distinct `idt(expr)` call site instead gets its
own auxiliary "accumulator" unknown `Y`, and lowering enforces `ddt(Y) = expr` through the
*same* charge-channel machinery `ddt` already uses — self-contained, exactly like a potential
contribution's own auxiliary branch-current unknown. Reading `idt(expr)`'s value anywhere in an
expression is then just an ordinary read of `Y`. This is why `idt` may appear nested arbitrarily
deep in an expression (a coefficient-scaled `idt` inside a potential contribution's RHS, the
shape `psp102`'s NQS variants need) while `ddt` may only appear as a top-level additive term —
`idt` reduces to reading a plain unknown, `ddt` doesn't.

**A `ddt` result assigned to a local variable and read back later** (a real, in-the-wild pattern:
some vendor models route around this project's own `ddt`-placement restriction by assigning it to
a scratch variable first) is tracked via a small, deliberately *not* general dataflow analysis:
forward, single-pass, branch-local — entering an `if`/`case` arm clones the tracking map and
discards the clone's mutations on exit, so a variable reused for something unrelated in only one
arm never leaks a stale `ddt` association to code after the branch.

## The code

```rust
// A ddt(...) argument routes to the charge channel instead of the resistive one:
sink.charge(row, q_value);
sink.dcharge(row, col, dq_dx);

// Each idt(expr) call site gets its own accumulator unknown Y, enforcing ddt(Y) = expr:
pub struct IdtAccumulator {
    // the idt(...) call's own ExprId.0 — how ad::Ctx::idt_slots maps a call site to its Y
    ...
}
```

`GeneratedModel::stamp_idt_accumulators` stamps every accumulator's own charge-channel row
unconditionally on every `load()` call, independent of whatever control flow does or doesn't
reach that specific `idt(...)` expression that step.

## It works

The generated capacitor stamps only the charge channel (`Q=C·V`, `dQ/dV=C`, an open circuit in
DC) and is checked against `va-abi::reference::Capacitor`'s own hand-written stamp —
`t2-codegen/02-lowering.qmd`'s AD-vs-reference milestone extended to a reactive device. The RC
transient milestone (`t4-transient/01-integration.qmd`) is the real end-to-end proof: a
generated capacitor's charge-channel output, differentiated by `va-transient`'s own companion
model, reproduces the analytic RC charging curve.

## Limitations

**No `.ic`/`UIC` initial-condition support** — shared honestly with every other reactive state in
this codegen and with `va-cli`'s own transient driver (`t4-transient/01-integration.qmd`,
`t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`'s SPICE-gotcha section has the concrete consequence this had
for QSPICE golden comparison).

**A dedicated "operator coverage matrix" for T2 specifically never got built as its own
artifact.** The roadmap originally scoped this phase to include one; what actually happened
instead is that coverage tracking consolidated into `docs/token-reference.md` (T1's living,
token-by-token record — since most real coverage gaps turned out to be frontend/elaboration
gaps, not codegen ones) and `docs/roadmap.md`'s own dated "Language coverage" changelog. That's
a real, honest gap in *this specific deliverable*, not a silently-abandoned promise — the
underlying tracking need is met, just not in the shape T2.3 originally called for.

## What's next

`t3-core/01-mna.qmd` — assembling every device's stamps into one linear system.
